title: Magnificent 7 Stocks 2026: Performance, Valuations, and W...
description: Is the stock market dangerously top-heavy? We analyze the Magnificent 7's staggering combined weight and valuations. The data exposes a massive concentration risk that could redefine your portfolio's future.

acceptedAnswer.text: The Magnificent 7 stocks refer to Apple (AAPL), Microsoft (MSFT), Alphabet (GOOGL), Amazon (AMZN), NVIDIA (NVDA), Meta Platforms (META), and Tesla (TSLA). These technology giants dominate the U.S. stock market in terms of market capitalization and influence.

mainEntity.name: What is the Mag 7 performance in 2026?

acceptedAnswer.text: The performance of the Mag 7 stocks varies by company, but collectively they continue to drive a significant portion of the broader market's returns. You can view their individual year-to-date (YTD) returns and revenue growth in our live data table above.

mainEntity.name: What is the magnificent seven weight in S&P 500?

acceptedAnswer.text: As of our latest data, the Magnificent Seven stocks account for approximately 32.24% of the entire S&P 500 index. This represents an unprecedented level of market concentration in just a handful of companies.

mainEntity.name: Is the S&P 500 too concentrated in the Mag 7?

acceptedAnswer.text: With the top 7 companies making up 32.24% of the index, the S&P 500 is highly concentrated. While this concentration has driven strong market returns during tech rallies, it also exposes passive index investors to significant concentration risk if the technology sector experiences a downturn.

mainEntity.name: How do Mag 7 valuations compare to the rest of the market?

acceptedAnswer.text: Historically, the Magnificent 7 stocks trade at a premium valuation (higher P/E ratios) compared to the rest of the S&P 500 due to their strong revenue growth, robust profit margins, and dominant market positions in high-growth areas like artificial intelligence and cloud computing.
